_comment: HO 416. Members whose active FEC committee is NOT reachable by sync-fec's chamber-derived candidate search, because their seated chamber differs from the office they are currently running for. All entries here are sitting House members (chamber='house') running for US Senate in 2026: resolveFecCandidateId keys office off members.chamber='house' and so re-finds their dormant House committee every run — the live Senate committee must be supplied explicitly. Keyed by bioguide (read from the members table, not typed from memory); fec_candidate_id is the live Senate 2026 id captured by read-only FEC candidate search during HO 416 classification. Consulted BEFORE the chamber-derived resolve in sync-fec.ts, mirroring how senate-special-elections.json is consulted before the class default in sync-members.ts (one source of truth for a member's effective FEC identity). Remove an entry when the member drops the Senate bid or leaves office. Product note: these are sitting Representatives — surfacing Senate-campaign fundraising on the member hub is a display decision, tracked in HO 416, not settled by this file.
name: Andy Barr

_comment: HO 416. Marshall is NOT a chamber-switcher — he is a sitting senator (chamber='senate') carrying his original House-era candidate_id H6KS01179 as a stale cache; his live Senate committee is S0KS00315. He needs the override for a different reason than the switchers above: BOTH ids classify as office=S and tie in scoreCandidate (last +1, first-prefix +3, incumbent +2 = 6), so the resolver's first-wins re-picks the stale H6 id — a plain re-resolve does NOT self-correct a scoring tie. Because chamber='senate', the member-hub Senate-campaign tag (derived from S-prefix id AND chamber='house') correctly does NOT apply to him; his line renders plain.
name: Roger Marshall
